key_points: Cooper Hall has had leaky pipes all year, fixed only with buckets
Housing portal crashes during critical application deadlines
Spring transfer students report being unassigned housing close to semester start
key_quotes: Cooper Hall is actual hell, unironic hell for a place that I'm giving over 2k to live in
Since the second week of school there have been leaky pipes in the laundry room. The only fix has been buckets
Still haven't been assigned housing and I'm a spring transfer for 2026

key_points: GHOE homecoming is a major event drawing national artists like Chloe Bailey
Greek life dominates social scene; non-Greek students can feel sidelined
Off-campus parties carry real safety risk from non-student locals
key_quotes: The locals do cause trouble on campus and that's why you have to be very safe when y'all go to house parties off campus
Them local dudes don't have anything going on and are looking for young girls they could impress
I've looked online but I'm not seeing much but parties, frats, and sororities

key_points: Dining hall described as 'mid' on weekends, students prefer cooking
Students at Sebastian dorm actively reducing meal plans to cook instead
Starship food delivery robots discontinued as of June 2026
key_quotes: The dining hall is kinda mid on weekends, so I'm thinking about limiting my meal plan so I can cook on the weekends
Due to Starship shutting down services in the United States, there will no longer be Starship bots on campus
Awww yall call it big cafe and mini cafe. All we had was Williams when I was there

key_points: Students navigating personal crises report little institutional safety net
One student nearly evicted waiting on delayed financial aid refund
Credit repeat policy shocks students already under academic and personal stress
key_quotes: I have been going through tough times through school, work, and my personal life. I am just 1 semester away from graduating
When do you think we'll get our refunds? (an aggie who is gonna be evicted in like 2 weeks without it)
I am just processing the shock

key_points: Financial aid letters arrive later than peer schools, causing planning anxiety
Out-of-state costs described as expensive with limited scholarship clarity
USDA 1890 full-ride scholarship available but students must seek it out themselves
key_quotes: I've logged on my portal but I can't find any financial letter. All my other colleges they both were sent together
I am an extremely low income. A full ride is my only way to go to NCAT
NC A&T gave students tuition assistance because of connections to staff, not merit or need: State Auditor

key_points: STEM reputation is strong; engineering and CS draw competitive transfers
Professors don't hand-hold — students must seek out office hours proactively
Transfer credit appeals are common and often frustrating for incoming students
key_quotes: NCAT are one of the top 3 in the AMIE Design Challenge out of 15 schools
No they do not treat you like a HS student because you are a big girl/boy at a university now. If you want help it's on you to search that out
I've already taken Calculus 1, 2, and 3 and received an A in all, however they are not being counted towards my degree

key_points: Academic advisors and distance learning staff frequently unresponsive to emails
Syllabus changed mid-semester by professors without clear communication
Major change requests denied without explanation, leaving students without recourse
key_quotes: How do you get the academic advisors or distance learning to respond about signing up for classes? I've emailed about 4 different people
The professor changed the syllabus mid-semester without clear communication
I keep getting denied for a major change for nursing and I literally don't know nor understand why

key_points: Greensboro is widely considered slow, especially for students from big cities
Campus itself is described as beautiful with strong community energy
Campus tours described as uninformative, barely beyond what Google shows
key_quotes: I'm from CHICAGO so to go from the raq to Greensboro? Lord
The campus is beautiful, all the people are beautiful and have smiles on their faces, the vibes are amazing
Our A&T end of 2024 tour we had was not moving. We learned nothing more than the internet showed. I am a disappointed Mama

key_points: Internship culture is strong in engineering; IBM, Toyota, Oak Ridge cited
Career fair mostly useful for seniors or students with prior internship experience
Alumni network is loyal and active — 'Aggies hire Aggies' sentiment is real
key_quotes: It was mandatory for Accounting and Engineering Majors to do Internships! I had an internship with IBM in my Junior Year
My general opinion is that the career fair is only good if you've got some internship before
The degree has taken me all over the world

key_points: In-state value is solid; out-of-state costs are a real concern without scholarships
Financial aid estimates in award letters may not match actual costs
Cooper Hall costs over $2k with documented maintenance failures
key_quotes: I'm out of state and I've heard how expensive it could get for out-of-state students
My offer says ~$20000 direct costs and $11000 worth of aid leaving $9000 out of pocket. Did you end up having to pay as much as they estimated?
Cooper Hall is actual hell, unironic hell for a place that I'm giving over 2k to live in

key_points: Community and belonging are the school's strongest selling points, consistently praised
Band, homecoming (GHOE), and Golden Delights are world-class cultural experiences
Students from PWIs transfer in specifically for the cultural environment
key_quotes: I've gotten into the community more and that's def the best part of going here
I realized that I wanted to experience that on a greater scale without the racism that comes with going to a predominantly white institution
One of the best decisions I made was choosing to be educated and surrounded by my people

key_points: Housing: Cooper Hall maintenance neglected all year with buckets catching leaks
Administration: Advisors routinely unresponsive to emails across multiple departments
Safety: Off-campus parties draw non-student locals who target female students
key_quotes: Since the second week of school, of the entire school year, there have been leaky pipes in the laundry room. The only fix has been buckets
I've emailed about 4 different people and no one responds
Them local dudes don't have anything going on and are looking for young girls they could impress

key_points: USDA 1890 full-ride scholarship covers tuition, housing, books, and internship
Band and Golden Delights are genuinely elite — recognized nationally by non-HBCU fans
Engineering alumni network actively places graduates in top companies globally
key_quotes: The USDA 1890 National Scholars program includes full tuition, books, fees, housing, and a summer internship
I'm from NYC and would love to see them perform at the Thanksgiving Day Parade — I don't think they get the credit they deserve
Accounting and Engineering Majors had mandatory internships. I had one with IBM in my Junior Year. AGGIE PRIDE!
